name: Halo: The Master Chief Collection
post_apply_message:
Halo MCC + AlphaRing (megabitt01 fork, Beta 1.3.3) — enables local SPLITSCREEN across all MCC games (a console feature the PC build lacks), plus Halo 3 camera/object tools and this fork's controller-to-player binding.
Apply installs: (1) AlphaRing's proxy DLL WTSAPI32.dll into steamapps/common/Halo The Master Chief Collection/mcc/binaries/win64 (any original is kept as .gfm-bak); (2) the launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="WTSAPI32=n,b" %command% on the Steam game (appid 976730) so Wine loads AlphaRing's WTSAPI32 from the game folder instead of the system one.

MANUAL STEP YOU MUST DO — launch MCC with ANTI-CHEAT DISABLED. EAC will not load an injected/proxy DLL, so AlphaRing is inert (and you can be kicked) unless anti-cheat is off. That means campaign / custom games / splitscreen only — no matchmaking. On the Deck, MCC otherwise runs EAC fine under Proton; you only turn it off to use this.

In game: open the AlphaRing menu with F4 (or Controller Back + Start), then use its Bind buttons to assign each controller to a player. For non-Xbox pads, turn on Steam Input + 'Xbox Configuration Support' in Steam settings so MCC sees them as 360 pads. VC++ redist that the Windows install guide lists is already bundled in GE/Proton, so no separate install.

Note: recipe pins AlphaRing 1.3.3's WTSAPI32.dll by sha256 (00474043…). If a newer AlphaRing release is wanted, bump the url + sha256 + size in remote_payloads.
notes:
Built 2026-07-13. Halo MCC = Steam-native (permanent appid 976730), so no non-Steam shortcut / gospel pin — its prefix restores by 976730 automatically. AlphaRing (github megabitt01/AlphaRing, fork of wouter51/AlphaRing) is a single-DLL modding tool: it ships as WTSAPI32.dll, a proxy for the Windows WTSAPI32 system DLL, dropped into mcc/binaries/win64 next to MCC-Win64-Shipping.exe. Install per its README: place the DLL, add WINEDLLOVERRIDES="WTSAPI32=n,b" %command% on Linux/Deck, launch with EAC disabled, toggle menu with F4 / Controller Back+Start. Purpose: PC splitscreen for all MCC titles + Halo 3 camera/object browsing; this fork adds controller-to-player Bind buttons and fixes default gamepad mapping (new profiles init with standard Xbox Halo controls). Ref video: youtube M26Mzan8SNM ('How To Play Halo Splitscreen on PC | AlphaRing Mod for MCC'). DLL fetched as a remote_payload (6.1MB, not committed) rather than into git. EAC-disabled is a manual in-launcher choice we can't force from a file step; flagged in post_apply_message. Untested on Tony's Deck as of build.

BLOCKED ON HARDWARE 2026-07-20 — NOT OUR BUG. Tony tested: MCC launches fine WITH anti-cheat, and refuses to launch with ANTI-CHEAT DISABLED. First suspicion was AlphaRing's proxy DLL, since EAC blocks it when enabled and loads it when disabled — exactly the difference between the two cases. Ruled out by reverting the recipe: with a STOCK, unmodified game folder, anti-cheat-disabled still refuses to launch.

So the EAC-disabled launch path is broken on this Deck independently of us, and AlphaRing never gets a chance to load. AlphaRing REQUIRES EAC off (EAC will not load an injected/proxy DLL), so this recipe cannot work until that launch path does. Nothing to fix in the recipe itself — the copy_files + launch_options steps applied correctly (log 11:33:45).

RELATED KNOWN ISSUE: 343/Valve documented a Steam Deck bug where selecting an anti-cheat launch option launches the OPPOSITE one. MCC's launch-option handling on Deck is unreliable in its own right, which is the likeliest neighbourhood for this.

NEXT LEAD (untested): anti-cheat-disabled just runs mcc/binaries/win64/MCC-Win64-Shipping.exe directly. If we can launch that exe inside MCC's own prefix we bypass the broken selector entirely — the usual trick is a launch option that rewrites the exe in %command%. Needs the exact default exe name from the live shortcut before it's worth writing as a step.

DO NOT re-litigate the fork question on this evidence: kirklandsig vs megabitt01 is irrelevant while no build can load at all.
